doc_description: A USDA Rural Development overview of the Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program, explaining no-money-down rural home loans, borrower and property eligibility, loan terms, and how to apply through approved lenders.

structure.title: Together, America Prospers
structure.summary:
The document explains USDA Rural Development’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide 100% financed, no-money-down home loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers. It describes what the program can be used for, including buying an existing home, building a new home, or financing repairs, improvements, or relocation of a home. It outlines borrower eligibility requirements such as living in the home as a primary residence, meeting local income limits, being unable to obtain conventional financing without PMI, and meeting citizenship and federal program requirements. It also covers property eligibility, including the need for homes to be in eligible rural areas and notes that some flipped properties may qualify.

The document further summarizes key loan terms: no down payment, no limit on gift funds, fixed 30-year interest rates, and loan amounts based on appraised value rather than purchase price. It mentions that closing costs and some related expenses may be included in financing. It explains how applicants must work through USDA-approved lenders and provides references to program rules, the handbook, and additional USDA resources.

nodes.title: Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program
nodes.summary: The document explains the USDA Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders offer 100% financed, no-money-down loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers in rural areas. It outlines what the program does, who can apply, and the property requirements, including primary residence use, income limits, inability to obtain conventional financing without PMI, and eligibility for single-family homes in qualifying rural locations. It also describes loan terms such as no down payment, fixed 30-year rates, flexible use of funds for purchase, repairs, closing costs, and related expenses, and notes that loans are based on repayment ability and appraised value rather than purchase price. The document also gives guidance on how to get started through USDA-approved lenders and lists the governing regulations and handbook resources.

nodes.title: What does this program do?
nodes.summary:
This document provides an overview of the USDA Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G). It explains that the program helps approved lenders offer 100% financed, no-money-down loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers, with USDA backing reducing lender risk. It describes what the loan can be used for, including buying an existing home, building a new home, or financing rehabilitation, improvements, or relocation costs.

The document also outlines who may apply, emphasizing primary residence occupancy, income eligibility, inability to obtain conventional financing without PMI, and meeting citizenship and federal program eligibility rules. It identifies eligible property requirements, such as being located in approved rural areas, and notes that there is no set maximum purchase price or acreage limit if the acreage is typical for the area.

In addition, it summarizes loan features like stable income requirements, no down payment, no limit on gift funds, fixed 30-year interest rates, and the ability to finance certain closing and related costs. It explains that applicants must work with approved USDA Rural Development lenders and references the governing regulations and handbook, along with links to program resources and local offices.

nodes.title: Who can apply?
nodes.summary: The document explains USDA’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide 100% financed, no-money-down loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers in rural areas. It describes what the program can be used for, including buying an existing home, building a new home, or funding repairs, improvements, or relocation of a home. It outlines borrower eligibility requirements such as primary residence use, income limits, inability to obtain conventional financing without PMI, and citizenship/status rules. It also covers property eligibility, including rural location requirements, loan terms like no down payment and fixed 30-year rates, and the fact that loans are based on appraised value rather than purchase price. Finally, it gives guidance on how to apply through USDA-approved lenders and references the governing regulations and handbook for the program.

nodes.title: What types of properties are eligible?
nodes.summary:
The document explains the USDA Rural Development Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide 100% financed, no-money-down home loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers. It outlines what the program does, who can apply, and the property requirements, emphasizing that buyers must live in the home as a primary residence, meet local income limits, and be unable to obtain conventional financing without PMI. It also notes that eligible properties must be in rural areas and that the program can be used to buy, build, repair, or relocate a home, including some “flipped” homes.

The document further describes key loan terms, such as no down payment, flexible use of gift funds, repayment based on ability to repay, and fixed 30-year interest rates negotiated with approved lenders. It explains that loans are based on appraised value rather than purchase price and may include certain related costs. Finally, it provides guidance on getting started through approved USDA lenders, lists the governing regulations and handbook, and directs readers to official USDA resources for updated information.

nodes.title: What are some other loan terms?
nodes.summary: The document explains USDA’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide 100% financed, no-money-down home loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers in rural areas. It describes who can qualify, including requirements to occupy the home as a primary residence, meet local income limits, and be unable to obtain conventional financing without private mortgage insurance. It also outlines eligible property types, such as existing homes, new construction, rehabilitation, and certain flipped properties, as long as they are in eligible rural locations. The document highlights key loan features like no down payment, no limit on gift funds, fixed 30-year interest rates, and the ability to finance certain closing and repair costs. It also tells applicants to work with USDA-approved lenders and provides references to program rules, handbooks, and additional USDA resources.

nodes.title: How do we get started?
nodes.summary: The document explains USDA’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide 100% financed, no-money-down home loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers in rural areas. It covers what the program does, who can apply, and property eligibility requirements, including income limits, primary residence rules, and the need to be unable to obtain conventional financing without PMI. It also describes key loan terms such as no down payment, possible use of gift funds, fixed 30-year rates, and financing based on appraised value rather than purchase price. The document notes that loans can be used to buy, build, repair, or relocate a home, and that eligible “flipped” properties may qualify. It ends with guidance on working with USDA-approved lenders and references the governing regulations and handbook for the program.

nodes.title: What governs this program?
nodes.summary:
This document explains USDA’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide 100% financed, no-money-down home loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers in rural areas. It covers what the program is for, who can apply, and the main eligibility requirements, such as using the home as a primary residence, meeting income limits, lacking conventional financing options without PMI, and meeting citizenship or legal status rules.

It also describes what types of properties qualify, including existing homes, home repairs or relocation costs, and new construction, as long as the property is in an eligible rural area. The document highlights key loan terms, such as no down payment, no limit on gift funds, fixed 30-year interest rates, and financing based on appraised value rather than purchase price. It notes that borrowers must work with approved USDA lenders and provides references to program rules, handbooks, and online resources.

pages.content:
Together , America Prospers 
Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program 
What does this 
program do? 
The Single Family Housing 
Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G) helps approved lenders offer 100 percent-fnanced, no-money-down loans to eligible low-and moderate-income people. 
Backed by USDA Rural 
Development, the 90-percent loan guarantee encourages lender participation by minimizing risk. 
With a Single Family Housing 
loan guarantee, you can buy an existing home, cover the cost of rehabilitating, improving, or relocating a home, or build new. 
This program is not restricted to 
frst-time homebuyers. 
QR code to the SFH-G webpage Who can apply? 
Among other requirements, you must: • Agree to occupy the home as your 
primary residence 
• Meet median household income requirements for the area in which the eligible home is located. You will fnd income eligibility information at this link: tinyurl.com/SFHG-income. 
• Be unable to get conventional fnancing with no private mortgage insurance (PMI) 
• Meet U.S. citizen or noncitizen requirements, and not be suspended or debarred from participating in federal programs 
What types of properties are eligible? 
Among other requirements, single-
family dwellings must be located in eligible rural areas. A property eligibility map is available at this link: tinyurl.com/SFHG-property. 
• Loans are based on repayment 
ability , and there is no set maximum purchase price or acreage limit as long as acreage is considered common for the area. 
• Provided they are otherwise eligible, “fipped” properties are allowed. What are some other loan terms? 
• You must have stable, dependable 
income and demonstrate a willingness and ability to repay debt. 
• No down payment is required, and there is no limit on gift funds. 
• Fixed, 30-year-interest rates are negotiated between you and your approved lender . 
• Loans are based on a property’s appraised value, not purchase price. Thus, you potentially can also include closing costs, home repair expenses, and such items as utilities connection fees, tax and insurance escrow, essential household equipment, and site preparation in your fnancing. 
How do we get started? 
• You must work directly with 
approved USDA RD lenders. A state-based list is available at this link: rd.usda.gov/resources/lenders 
What governs this program? 
• 7-CFR Part 3555, available at this 
link: tinyurl.com/7CFR-3555 
• The SFH-G Handbook is available at this link: tinyurl.com/SFHG-HB - PDF 
NOTE: Because information changes, always consult offcial program instructions 
or contact your local Rural Development offce for help. A list is available at this link: go.usa.gov/xJHPE. You will fnd additional resources, forms, and program information at rd.usda.gov. USDA is an equal opportunity provider, employer, and lender. 
Last revised October 2024
